techpack.item.tooltip2.moddedpedia: Right-click to open the guide window
gui.techpack.machines.advanced_tier.ener: - 25% More energy cost than basic tier
gui.techpack.machines.sophisticated_tier: - 50% More energy cost than basic tier
tip.techpack.portable_fluid_capsule.text: Portable Fluid Capsule its an item that allow you to extract up to 1,000 mB of fluid from inside blocks. This is very useful for cleaning broken values.
tip.techpack.fluid_cells.text: Fluid Cells retain the fluids in their inventory even if you break them, facilitating fluid transport.
tip.techpack.heat_dynamo.text: Heat Dynamos generate small amounts of energy passively when connected to a heat source (Pneumaticcraft) of 500 degrees Celsius or higher.
tip.techpack.cryo_dynamo.text: Cryo Dynamos generate small amounts of energy passively when connected to a heat source (Pneumaticcraft) of -100 degrees Celsius or less.
tip.techpack.machines.text: Techpack Machines store their items, energy, fluids, and other components when they are broken.
tip.techpack.curiosity_1.text: Originally, the tech pack was intended for version 1.20.1 and would have had significantly less polish, but the performance gains of version 1.21.1 were a major selling point.
tip.techpack.curiosity_2.text: The developer's interest in expert modpacks began with an E2EE series by a Brazilian YouTuber named ArtpokeBR, who is now a developer at FTB.
tip.techpack.curiosity_3.text: The YouTuber ArtpokeBR was one of the main indirect contributors to the developer getting a PC.
tip.techpack.curiosity_4.text: Industrial Craft, especially the classic edition, is one of the major inspirations for the Techpack machines.
tip.techpack.curiosity_5.text: The idea of merging concepts from old mods and reinterpreting them emerged after the project's development resumed in January 2026.
tip.techpack.curiosity_6.text: Raspberry Flavored was crucial in achieving the aesthetically polished TechPack.
tip.techpack.curiosity_7.text: StarCorp was the company responsible for sending the player to the past, shortly before a catastrophe occurred in the future.
